title: Calytrix brownii
intro: Calytrix brownii, also known as Brown's fringe flower, is a small shrub native to Western Australia, belonging to the family Myrtaceae. The plant has been traditionally used by Indigenous Australians to treat various health issues, including coughs, colds, and sore throats, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Bioactive constituents of Calytrix brownii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Calytrix brownii is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures to help alleviate respiratory issues and promote general well-being.
